title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding
task: 3D scene understanding and spatial reasoning for MLLMs
key_results: Outperforms state-of-the-art on 3D scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation benchmarks without explicit 3D supervision
comments: Clever repurposing of video diffusion models' implicit spatial priors; demonstrates that generative models learn robust physical priors as a byproduct of temporal coherence.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video generation models learn implicit 3D structural priors from temporal coherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; extracted spatiotemporal features improve MLLM spatial reasoning

title: SAMA: Factorized Semantic Anchoring and Motion Alignment for Instruction-Guided Video Editing
task: Instruction-guided video editing with semantic-motion factorization
key_results: Achieves state-of-the-art among open-source and competitive with commercial systems; factorized pre-training alone yields strong zero-shot editing
comments: The factorization paradigm elegantly separates semantic understanding from temporal dynamics, avoiding explicit external priors that limit generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoupling semantic anchoring and motion modeling improves editing quality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot ability validates the factorization approach

title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
task: Real-time vision-language-action inference with low latency
key_results: Compresses immediate reaction denoising tenfold into single step; enables real-time robotics on consumer GPUs with table-tennis task validation
comments: Addresses a practical bottleneck in VLA deployment; Horizon-Aware Schedule is elegant but relies on action chunking assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constant sampling schedules are inefficient for reaction latency in flow-based VLAs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; adaptive prioritization of near-term actions reduces reaction latency

title: 3DreamBooth: High-Fidelity 3D Subject-Driven Video Generation Model
task: 3D-aware subject-driven video generation
key_results: Achieves view-consistent video generation of customized 3D subjects via 1-frame optimization; enables multi-view adaptation without exhaustive video training
comments: Novel insight that single-frame optimization with geometric conditioning can avoid temporal overfitting; asymmetrical multi-view strategy is well-motivated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Restricting spatial updates in 1-frame optimization prevents temporal overfitting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; robust 3D priors learned without multi-view video data

title: Bridging Semantic and Kinematic Conditions with Diffusion-based Discrete Motion Tokenizer
task: Human motion generation with semantic and kinematic control
key_results: Reduces trajectory error from 0.72 to 0.08 cm and FID from 0.083 to 0.029 on HumanML3D using one-sixth of tokens; improves under stronger constraints
comments: Bridging discrete tokens and continuous diffusion is elegant; the three-stage framework cleanly separates concerns.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Delegating motion recovery to diffusion decoder enables compact tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; single-layer tokens preserve fidelity with 83% token reduction

title: MonoArt: Progressive Structural Reasoning for Monocular Articulated 3D Reconstruction
task: Monocular articulated 3D object reconstruction
key_results: State-of-the-art on PartNet-Mobility in reconstruction accuracy and inference speed; generalizes to robotic manipulation and articulated scenes
comments: Progressive structural reasoning avoids direct articulation regression; missing quantitative results and comparison metrics in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Progressive transformation into canonical geometry enables stable articulation inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms multi-view and retrieval-based baselines

title: Cubic Discrete Diffusion: Discrete Visual Generation on High-Dimensional Representation Tokens
task: Discrete visual generation on high-dimensional representation tokens
key_results: First discrete generation model for high-dimensional (768-1024 dim) tokens; state-of-the-art on ImageNet-256 with strong scaling (900M to 3.7B); validates discretized tokens preserve original capabilities
comments: Enabling unified multimodal architectures via discrete high-dim tokens is ambitious; fine-grained masking approach is well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-dimensional discrete tokens can preserve representation capabilities for both understanding and generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; same tokens effective for dual tasks

title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation
task: Post-training compact MoE LLMs with cascade RL
key_results: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (30B MoE, 3B active) achieves Gold in IMO, IOI, ICPC with 20x fewer parameters than DeepSeekV3.2; competitive with frontier models
comments: Multi-domain on-policy distillation during RL is clever for recovering regressions; exceptional density-to-capability ratio.

model_io.model: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (MoE with cascade RL + multi-domain distillation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cascade RL + multi-domain on-policy distillation sustains gains in compact MoE models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong reasoning with high parameter efficiency

title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs
task: Evaluating omnimodal LLMs on long-form audio-video understanding
key_results: Gemini 3 Pro achieves ~65% accuracy; open-source models <35%; reveals challenges in long-term memory, temporal localization, and multimodal perception
comments: Critical gap identified between short-clip and real-world long-form video; benchmark is valuable but shows existing models are far from production-ready.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current OmniLLMs struggle with long-form audio-visual understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant accuracy gaps across all tested models

title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents
task: Continual agent self-improvement via skill design
key_results: 26.2% and 116.2% relative accuracy improvements on General AI Assistants and Humanity's Last Exam; agent autonomously designs agents for new tasks
comments: Memory-based RL with externalised skills enables parameter-free adaptation; impressive but lacks analysis of failure modes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents can autonomously design task-specific agents through iterative skill refinement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; sustained gains across benchmarks

title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World
task: Multilingual embedding models for 200+ languages
key_results: F2LLM-v2-14B ranks first on 11 MTEB benchmarks; smaller models achieve SOTA for resource-constrained settings; supports 200+ languages including low-resource
comments: Matryoshka learning + pruning + distillation is effective recipe; emphasis on underserved languages is important.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two-stage LLM-based training with matryoshka learning enables efficient multilingual embeddings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA across diverse model sizes

title: ReactMotion: Generating Reactive Listener Motions from Speaker Utterance
task: Generate reactive listener body motions from speaker utterances
key_results: ReactMotion outperforms retrieval baselines and LLM pipelines on naturalness, diversity, and appropriateness metrics; dataset ReactMotionNet contains speaker-listener motion pairs with multi-level appropriateness annotations
comments: The preference-based training approach is well-motivated for modeling the inherent non-determinism of human reactions, though generalization beyond the dataset domain remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preference-based training enables more appropriate and diverse listener motion generation than single-ground-truth supervision
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ReactMotion outperforms baselines in appropriateness and diversity

title: Cognitive Mismatch in Multimodal Large Language Models for Discrete Symbol Understanding
task: Evaluating MLLMs on discrete symbol understanding
key_results: Models fail at basic symbol recognition but succeed at complex reasoning; open-source models <35%, frontier models ~65-75%; reveals cognitive mismatch
comments: Uncovers fundamental limitation: MLLMs rely on linguistic probability rather than true visual perception of symbols; important diagnostic.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLMs exhibit cognitive mismatch in discrete symbol understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; fail basic recognition but succeed at complex reasoning

title: AndroTMem: From Interaction Trajectories to Anchored Memory in Long-Horizon GUI Agents
task: Long-horizon GUI interaction with effective memory mechanisms for Android tasks
key_results: Anchored State Memory (ASM) improves Task Complete Rate by 5%-30.16% over full-sequence and summary baselines on 1,069 tasks; identifies within-task memory failures as primary performance bottleneck
comments: The diagnostic framework effectively isolates memory as the limiting factor in long-horizon GUI agents, though ASM's reliance on identifying critical intermediate states may require task-specific tuning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Performance degradation in long-horizon tasks is primarily due to memory failures rather than perception or action errors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across 12 GUI agents; ASM addresses this bottleneck

title: EffectErase: Joint Video Object Removal and Insertion for High-Quality Effect Erasing
task: Video object removal with effect erasing and consistent background synthesis
key_results: VOR dataset: 60K paired videos, 5 effect types; EffectErase achieves superior performance in effect-aware removal via reciprocal insertion-removal learning
comments: Dataset addresses real gap in video inpainting; treating insertion as auxiliary task is well-motivated for learning complementary behaviors.

outputs: video with object removed and background restored

hypotheses.hypothesis: Insertion as auxiliary task improves removal quality through complementary learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; insertion-removal consistency objective enhances effect erasing

title: Tinted Frames: Question Framing Blinds Vision-Language Models
task: Improve visual grounding in Vision-Language Models through prompt tuning
key_results: Constrained question framing (yes/no, multiple choice) reduces visual attention by substantial margins; learnable token prompts restore visually grounded attention patterns and improve cross-framing consistency
comments: The mechanistic finding that VLMs modulate attention based on linguistic framing is insightful, but the lightweight tuning method's effectiveness on more complex vision-reasoning tasks beyond the tested scope remains open.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Question framing causes selective attention blindness in VLMs, with constrained framings inducing lower image attention
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via attention quantification; learnable tokens mitigate this effect

title: SimulU: Training-free Policy for Long-form Simultaneous Speech-to-Speech Translation
task: Training-free policy for long-form simultaneous speech-to-speech translation
key_results: SimulU achieves comparable quality-latency trade-off to cascaded baselines without training on MuST-C across 8 languages; first training-free approach for long-form SimulS2S
comments: Leveraging cross-attention in pre-trained models for policy derivation is elegant but the approach's robustness to model architecture variations and truly long-form scenarios (>30 minutes) requires further validation.

outputs: translated speech stream with controlled latency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-attention patterns in pre-trained end-to-end models can be exploited to formulate effective simultaneous translation policies without additional training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SimulU matches cascaded baselines on quality-latency tradeoffs

title: VTC-Bench: Evaluating Agentic Multimodal Models via Compositional Visual Tool Chaining
task: Evaluating agentic MLLMs on tool composition and visual reasoning
key_results: Gemini-3.0-Pro achieves 51% on VTC-Bench; reveals struggles in tool generalization and multi-tool composition; 680 problems across 9-category hierarchy
comments: Comprehensive benchmark with realistic OpenCV tools; exposes that models rely on familiar subsets rather than selecting optimal tools.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with generalization to diverse tool-sets and multi-tool composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; limited to familiar tools, inefficient planning

title: MOSS-TTS Technical Report
task: Speech generation with token-based autoregressive modeling
key_results: Supports zero-shot voice cloning, token-level duration control, pronunciation control, code-switching, long-form generation; two model variants for scalability vs. efficiency
comments: Discrete audio tokens + causal tokenizer is sound recipe; variable-bitrate RVQ for semantic-acoustic coupling is well-designed.

model_io.model: MOSS-TTS (autoregressive speech generator on discrete tokens)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal tokenizer with variable-bitrate RVQ enables unified semantic-acoustic representation for zero-shot voice cloning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; supports multilingual and long-form generation

title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
task: Scalable rollout infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents
key_results: ProRL Agent enables RL training on software engineering, math, STEM, and coding; supports rootless HPC environments; rollout-as-a-service design decouples orchestration
comments: Infrastructure contribution; clean separation of concerns (rollout vs. training) improves maintainability but limited novelty in RL itself.

model_io.model: ProRL Agent (RL infrastructure for agentic rollouts)

title: OSM-based Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing VLMs
task: Domain adaptation of VLMs for remote sensing without teacher models
key_results: OSMDA-VLM achieves SOTA on 10 benchmarks; eliminates dependency on frontier teacher models; generates annotations via OSM pairing without manual labels
comments: Self-contained framework using OSM as annotation engine is clever and scalable; demonstrates that auxiliary geographic data replaces need for stronger teachers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: OSM-based pseudo-labeling enables domain adaptation without external teacher models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with lower training cost

title: Loc3R-VLM: Language-based Localization and 3D Reasoning with Vision-Language Models
task: Language-based localization and 3D reasoning with monocular video
key_results: SOTA on language-based localization and 3D QA benchmarks; outperforms 2D and video baselines via global layout + egocentric perspective supervision
comments: Joint objectives for layout reconstruction and situation modeling elegantly ground language in 3D; lightweight camera pose priors from foundation models is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global layout + egocentric supervision enables VLMs to reason about 3D space
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA on localization and 3D QA tasks

title: MHPO: Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization for Stable Reinforcement Learning
task: Stable reinforcement learning with hazard-aware policy optimization
key_results: MHPO outperforms existing methods on reasoning benchmarks (text and vision-language); Log-Fidelity Modulator enables differentiable ratio control; asymmetric hazard penalties prevent mode collapse
comments: Survival analysis for hazard modeling is creative; addresses real instability issues in GRPO but lacks comparison with recent PPO variants.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Log-Fidelity Modulator + Decoupled Hazard Penalty improve training stability and prevent mode collapse
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent gains on diverse benchmarks

title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation
task: Mathematical reasoning with formal object derivation and test-time aggregation
key_results: Strong LMs (Qwen3-235B, o3) struggle on Principia; on-policy judge training boosts performance; test-time aggregation scales compute; cross-format generalization to numerical/MCQA
comments: Principia benchmark addresses real gap in formal reasoning; on-policy judge training is well-motivated but evaluation limited to few models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On-policy judge training improves mathematical object reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; boosts performance on Principia and numerical tasks

title: Matryoshka Gaussian Splatting
task: Level-of-detail rendering for 3D Gaussian Splatting
key_results: Continuous LoD without sacrificing full-capacity quality; stochastic budget training maintains backbone performance; smooth speed-quality trade-off from single model
comments: Clever stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without architecture changes; useful for practical 3D applications.

model_io.model: MGS (ordered Gaussian splatting with stochastic training)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without quality degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; matches full-capacity performance across budgets

title: What Really Controls Temporal Reasoning in Large Language Models: Tokenisation or Representation of Time?
task: Temporal reasoning across languages and calendar systems
key_results: Tokenisation fragmentation is the bottleneck in low-resource languages; temporal linearity predicts reasoning in high-resource settings; accuracy collapses in rare calendar formats due to Year/Month/Day separation disruption
comments: Well-designed benchmark isolating tokenisation vs. representation effects, though the causal claims about 'what controls' reasoning rest on correlational mixed-effects analysis.

inputs: multilingual temporal questions (date arithmetic, timezone conversion, relation extraction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tokenisation quality of temporal artefacts is a resource-dependent bottleneck
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; fragmentation disrupts accuracy in low-resource languages and rarer calendar formats

hypotheses.hypothesis: Temporal linearity is the strongest predictor of reasoning in high-resource languages
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via crossed mixed-effects regression

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fragmentation is the stronger predictor in low-resource languages
hypotheses.result: Confirmed

title: Prompt-Free Universal Region Proposal Network
task: Prompt-free universal object proposal generation
key_results: Works with 5% of COCO data; generalizes to 19 datasets without fine-tuning (underwater, industrial defect, remote sensing); no external prompts required
comments: Learnable query embeddings with visual feature coupling and cascade self-prompting is effective; impressive zero-shot generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-prompted learnable embeddings enable prompt-free object detection generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot transfer across domains

title: Mending the Holes: Mitigating Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Translation
task: Low-resource multilingual machine translation via RL with quality estimation
key_results: WALAR improves 1400 language directions on Flores-101; outperforms LLaMAX; retains high-resource performance while elevating low-resource translation
comments: Insight about QE model failure modes ('holes') motivating word/language alignment is practical; RL-only approach using monolingual data is resource-efficient.

model_io.model: LLM with RL (QE-based reward, word/language alignment)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitigating QE model holes via word/language alignment improves low-resource translation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; large gains on 1400 Flores-101 directions

title: DreamPartGen: Semantically Grounded Part-Level 3D Generation via Collaborative Latent Denoising
task: Semantically grounded part-level 3D object generation from text
key_results: State-of-the-art geometric fidelity and text-shape alignment; framework enables coherent, interpretable synthesis with explicit part semantics and inter-part relations
comments: Novel approach combining geometry and semantics via latent co-denoising is conceptually sound, but abstract lacks concrete quantitative benchmarks or ablation details.

model_io.model: DreamPartGen (diffusion-based with Duplex Part Latents and Relational Semantic Latents)
outputs: semantically grounded 3D objects with part decomposition

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint modeling of part geometry and appearance with inter-part semantic relations improves 3D generation fidelity
hypotheses.result: Achieved state-of-the-art performance, though specific improvements not quantified in abstract

title: PARSA-Bench: A Comprehensive Persian Audio-Language Model Benchmark
task: Evaluate audio-language models on Persian language and cultural understanding
key_results: PARSA-Bench comprises 16 tasks with 8,000+ samples; text-only baselines outperform audio models; all models achieve near-random performance on prosodic vazn detection
comments: The benchmark effectively exposes a critical gap in prosodic perception across model scales, highlighting a fundamental limitation of current audio-language models rather than a training or dataset issue.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current audio-language models do not effectively leverage audio-specific information beyond what transcription provides
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; text-only baselines consistently outperform audio counterparts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prosodic understanding (vazn detection) is beyond reach of current models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; all models perform near random on culturally-grounded prosodic tasks

title: VID-AD: A Dataset for Image-Level Logical Anomaly Detection under Vision-Induced Distraction
task: Image-level logical anomaly detection under visual distraction in manufacturing
key_results: VID-AD dataset contains 10,395 images across 50 one-class tasks with controlled visual distraction; language-based framework with contrastive learning outperforms baselines by capturing logical attributes over low-level features
comments: Using text descriptions of normal images to synthesize negative examples via contradiction is a clever data augmentation approach, but the framework's transferability to novel manufacturing scenarios beyond the 10 included remains untested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contrastive learning on logical attributes (vs. low-level visual features) improves robustness to vision-induced distraction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; text-based embeddings improve performance across distraction conditions

title: COT-FM: Cluster-wise Optimal Transport Flow Matching
task: Faster flow matching via cluster-wise optimal transport
key_results: COT-FM accelerates sampling and improves generation quality; straightens vector fields via cluster-wise coupling; plug-and-play across 2D, image, and robotics tasks
comments: Cluster-wise OT to improve flow straightness is intuitive; no architectural changes required, making adoption practical.

model_io.model: COT-FM (flow matching with optimal transport reshaping)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster-wise OT yields straighter vector fields and faster convergence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent acceleration and quality improvement

title: Structural basis for prostaglandin and drug transport via SLCO2A1
task: Determine structural basis for prostaglandin and NSAID transport selectivity via SLCO2A1 transporter
key_results: Cryo-EM structures of SLCO2A1 bound to endogenous prostaglandins and four NSAIDs; molecular basis for substrate and drug recognition elucidated
comments: Structures rationalize substrate selectivity within SLCO superfamily, though quantitative binding affinity (Kd) values and kinetic rate constants would strengthen mechanistic claims.

inputs: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, indomethacin, and one other)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prostaglandins and NSAIDs share common recognition elements at SLCO2A1 binding site
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via structural analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: SLCO2A1 structure explains subfamily adaptation within SLCO superfamily
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mechanistic details identify divergent selectivity

title: Structural mechanism of substrate binding and inhibition of human taurine transporter
task: Determine substrate binding mechanism and conformational states of human taurine transporter
key_results: Four distinct structural states resolved (substrate-free, taurine-bound, β-alanine-bound, P4S-bound); TM1a helix bending facilitates intracellular gate opening
comments: High-resolution structures provide clear mechanistic insights, though functional validation of inhibitor selectivity in cells would strengthen therapeutic claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: β-alanine and P4S occupy the same substrate-binding site as taurine
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via structural overlay

hypotheses.hypothesis: TM1a bending enables ion/substrate release in inward-open state
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in substrate-free and P4S-bound conformations

title: A single cluster of RNA Polymerase II molecules is stably associated with active genes
task: Characterize RNA Polymerase II cluster dynamics and functional association with active genes during zygotic genome activation
key_results: RNAPII cluster lifetimes depend on transcriptional activity; single clusters stably associate with active loci; cluster intensity correlates strongly with transcriptional output (r² implied high correlation)
comments: Excellent single-molecule tracking approach with developmental timing context, though phase-separation mechanisms cannot be fully ruled out without additional biophysical validation.

model_io.model: Drosophila melanogaster embryo during ZGA with RNAPII fluorescent labeling
inputs: single-molecule trajectories via lattice light-sheet microscopy

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNAPII clusters form via higher-order phase separation
hypotheses.result: Refuted; data and simulations support local polymerase accumulation model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster intensity correlates with transcriptional activity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong correlation observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster lifetimes depend on transcription initiation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lifetime increases with activity

title: Dielectric double shell characterization of yeast cells exposed to simulated microgravity
task: Characterize microgravity-induced changes in yeast cell membrane properties using dielectrophoresis
key_results: Simulated microgravity reduces membrane folding factor; significant changes in membrane permittivity and conductivity; impaired nutrient uptake
comments: Dielectrophoresis provides sensitive biophysical readout, but 24-h exposure timeframe and clinostat simulation may not fully replicate organismal microgravity response.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Microgravity alters yeast cell membrane properties
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced folding factor and altered permittivity/conductivity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Membrane changes impair nutrient uptake and metabolism
hypotheses.result: Inferred from membrane conductivity reduction

title: Soft bionic actuation explains the functional role of whisking in seal whisker sensing
task: Elucidate functional role of active whisking in seal hydrodynamic prey detection
key_results: Undulated whiskers show 3-fold lower vortex-induced vibrations and 50-fold higher SNR than sea lion whiskers; active whisking with soft actuators enables high-sensitivity prey tracking
comments: Elegant integration of bionic actuation with natural whiskers bridges morphology and behavior, though in vivo validation of SNR thresholds for actual prey detection remains limited.

model_io.model: Bionic seal muzzle with 30 natural whiskers + electrohydraulic soft actuators

hypotheses.hypothesis: Undulatory morphology suppresses self-induced vibrations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 3-fold VIV reduction vs smooth whiskers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Active whisking increases sensing performance SNR
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 50-fold SNR enhancement with rhythmic protraction

title: A computational analysis of biophysical and geometric constraints refutes existing hypotheses of cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia
task: Test biophysical mechanisms explaining cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia neurons
key_results: No single proposed mechanism (electrical coupling, gap junctions, glial-mediated) adequately explains cross-excitation; likely multifactorial process
comments: Valuable negative result highlighting a genuine gap in sensory neuroscience, but computational constraints or unmeasured mechanisms may limit mechanistic inference.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Classical neuron-neuron electrical coupling explains cross-excitation
hypotheses.result: Refuted; glial sheaths preclude sufficient coupling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glial-mediated ion or transmitter spillover explains phenomenon
hypotheses.result: Insufficient alone; requires additional mechanisms

title: FAK/SRC-JNK axis promotes ferroptosis via upregulating ACSL4 expression
task: Identify signaling axis regulating ferroptosis and test therapeutic potential
key_results: FAK/SRC-JNK pathway upregulates ACSL4 to promote ferroptosis; defactinib (FAK inhibitor) suppresses ferroptosis and protects against acute pancreatitis
comments: Interesting dual therapeutic application (sensitizing cancer cells vs. protecting from pancreatitis), though the opposing effects of different JNK transcription factors suggest complex regulation requiring further mechanistic clarity.

model_io.model: cancer cells and acute pancreatitis models

hypotheses.hypothesis: FAK/SRC-JNK signaling promotes ferroptosis through ACSL4 upregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; JNK transcription factors (ATF2, NFATC1/3, SMAD4) activate ACSL4 while others (c-Jun, STAT3, ELK1, HSF1) repress it

title: Loss of Pum2 exacerbates colitis by disrupting macrophage–epithelial crosstalk and promoting epithelial necroptosis
task: Identify Pum2 as regulator of macrophage-epithelial crosstalk in colitis pathogenesis
key_results: UC patients show reduced Pum2 expression inversely correlated with disease activity; Pum2 deficiency exacerbates DSS-colitis through macrophage hyperactivation and TNFα-dependent epithelial necroptosis
comments: RNA-binding protein Pum2 emerges as therapeutic target; specific Pum2-regulated transcripts controlling macrophage activation or TNFα production warrant identification.

model_io.model: UC patient colonic tissue, DSS-induced colitis in WT and Pum2 KO mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pum2 loss exacerbates colitis by promoting macrophage-epithelial pathogenic interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Pum2 deficiency increases macrophage inflammation and TNFα-dependent epithelial necroptosis in DSS-colitis

title: A cell cycle-dependent transition of acetylation to phosphorylation regulates timely centrosome maturation
task: Elucidate cell cycle control of centrosome maturation timing
key_results: CDK1-mediated phosphorylation of RNF40 at T529/T557 in G2/M recruits PLK1 and activates centrosome maturation; acetylation-to-phosphorylation transition primes the cascade
comments: Elegant mechanistic study revealing post-translational modification switching as a temporal control mechanism; constitutive acetylation causes mitotic catastrophe, validating the model.

model_io.model: synchronized mammalian cells, cell cycle phases

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNF40 modification state controls timing of centrosome maturation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; acetylation in interphase blocks CDK1-RNF40-PLK1 cascade; G2/M phosphorylation enables it

title: AP-1 mediates cellular adaptation and memory formation
task: Demonstrate cellular memory formation and AP-1-dependent encoding during drug resistance development
key_results: Cancer cells exposed to subtherapeutic doses adapt and develop drug resistance with encoded cellular memory; AP-1 transcription factor maintains memory; chromatin accessibility persists; two-color reporter shows cis-encoded memory
comments: Novel concept of AP-1-mediated cis epigenetics in cellular learning; limited understanding of how transient gene expression becomes permanently encoded or erased, and whether this applies to other cell types.

model_io.model: cancer cells during therapy exposure and resistance development

hypotheses.hypothesis: AP-1 encodes cellular memories during stress adaptation via cis epigenetics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; low-dose therapy induces transient expression that becomes stably encoded; two-color AP-1 reporter shows cis localization

title: Mitorubin, berberrubine-based compounds that improve mitochondrial function, exhibit cardioprotective effects against age-related cardiac dysfunction
task: Develop MITOL-activating compounds to restore age-related cardiac mitochondrial dysfunction
key_results: Berberrubine-based Mitorubin compounds enhance MITOL expression, increase mitochondrial DNA and protein content, restore cardiac function, and extend lifespan in HFD-fed mice without shortening lifespan in aged mice
comments: Strong preclinical evidence for a novel mitochondrial activator; water-soluble acetic acid derivative addresses formulation challenges, though human efficacy remains untested.

model_io.model: cultured cells, age-related cardiac dysfunction and HFD models in mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: MITOL activation improves age-related cardiac mitochondrial function
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Mitorubin restores mitochondrial function and cardiac performance

title: Visualizing suborganellar lipid distribution using correlative light and electron microscopy
task: Develop correlative light and electron microscopy workflow for nanoscale lipid localization
key_results: Lipid-CLEM enables quantification of lipid densities in membrane nanodomains; reveals differential sphingomyelin partitioning into intraluminal vesicles, recycling tubules, and early endosome boundary membrane
comments: Novel methodological contribution with immediate biological application; demonstrates lipid organization at nanoscale previously observed only for proteins, expanding understanding of membrane nanodomain architecture.

model_io.model: cellular membranes and endosomal compartments imaged by Lipid-CLEM

title: Melanin concentrating hormone regulates bone cell activities and calcium metabolism in regenerating goldfish scales
task: Characterize MCH regulation of bone cell activities and calcium metabolism during scale regeneration
key_results: Single MCH treatment suppresses osteoclast genes and reduces plasma calcium; prolonged MCH elevates both osteoblast and osteoclast markers with decreased scale calcium; MCH receptor 2 mediates effects
comments: Complex dose-dependent and time-dependent effects suggest distinct mechanisms for acute vs. chronic MCH signaling; the functional outcome of simultaneous osteoblast/osteoclast activation during prolonged treatment remains unclear.

model_io.model: cultured goldfish scales, whole organism goldfish

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single MCH treatment inhibits osteoclasts to reduce plasma calcium
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TRAP activity and osteoclast genes suppressed 24h post-treatment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prolonged MCH affects both osteoblasts and osteoclasts with net decrease in scale mineralization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; continuous treatment elevates both cell markers but reduces scale calcium content

title: Hypoxia-induced gene expression changes in N. vectensis embryos
task: Characterize embryonic hypoxia responses in early-diverging metazoan
key_results: N. vectensis embryogenesis is O2-dependent with reversible hypoxia-induced developmental arrest; transcriptomics show conserved hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways shared with bilaterians
comments: Important evolutionary study establishing that hypoxia adaptation toolkit was present in cnidarian-bilaterian ancestor; limited mechanistic insight into specific developmental arrests or adaptive responses.

model_io.model: N. vectensis embryos exposed to hypoxia

hypotheses.hypothesis: Early metazoan embryonic hypoxia responses are evolutionarily conserved
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; core hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways match bilaterian patterns

title: Thioredoxin protects against diabetic hearing loss by regulating TOMM22 mediated mitochondrial autophagy in hair cells and inhibiting microglial M1 polarization
task: Elucidate mechanisms by which thioredoxin protects against diabetic hearing loss
key_results: Trx1 overexpression suppresses apoptosis and mitophagy via TOMM22/PINK1 axis and shifts microglia to M2 phenotype; elevated serum Trx associated with reduced OAE parameters as potential biomarker
comments: Dual-mechanism protection (cell-autonomous mitochondrial and non-autonomous immunomodulatory) is well-supported; the biomarker association seems counterintuitive and warrants clinical validation.

model_io.model: diabetic mouse cochlea, AGE-induced HEI-OC1 cells, microglia coculture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx protects hearing by maintaining TOMM22 and suppressing mitophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CCCP-mediated TOMM22 inhibition reverses Trx1 protection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx promotes microglial M2 polarization to improve auditory microenvironment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; conditioned medium from Trx-treated microglia protects HEI-OC1 cells

title: Collagen-specific molecular chaperone Hsp47 in inguinal white adipose tissue promotes high-fat diet-induced inflammatory gene expression in male mice
task: Determine depot-specific role of Hsp47 in HFD-induced adipose inflammation
key_results: Adipocyte-specific Hsp47 knockout reduces adipocyte size, fibrosis, and pro-inflammatory genes specifically in inguinal (subcutaneous) WAT, not epididymal WAT; serum metabolic markers improve
comments: Depot-specific effects highlight important tissue heterogeneity in obesity; the mechanism linking collagen-Hsp47 interactions to inflammation requires further elucidation.

model_io.model: WT and Hsp47 aKO mice on HFD for 12 weeks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Elevated Hsp47 in ingWAT during HFD promotes inflammation and dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Hsp47 aKO reduces inflammation and fibrosis in ingWAT but not epiWAT

title: ROS-NLRP3 participates in the pyroptosis response of excretory-secretory products from protoscoleces of Echinococcus granulosus in hepatocytes
task: Determine mechanisms of hepatocyte injury by E. granulosus parasitic products via pyroptosis
key_results: ESP exposure triggers ROS-dependent NLRP3 inflammasome activation and pyroptosis in AML-12 hepatocytes; MCC950 (NLRP3 inhibitor) and NAC (ROS scavenger) suppress pyroptosis and cytotoxicity
comments: Clear mechanistic pathway established (ROS→NLRP3→pyroptosis); however, the identity of ROS-generating source and upstream triggers remain uncharacterized.

model_io.model: AML-12 hepatocytes exposed to E. granulosus ESPs

hypotheses.hypothesis: ESP-induced hepatocyte pyroptosis occurs via ROS-NLRP3 inflammasome pathway
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NLRP3 inhibition and ROS scavenging both suppress pyroptosis

title: The redundant protein synthesis gene Aimp1 challenges the canonical inverse relationship between translation and autophagy
task: Identify and characterize redundant protein synthesis genes that limit autophagy
key_results: Aimp1 depletion uncouples mTORC1 activity and increases autophagy with minimal effect on protein synthesis; during immune responses, Aimp1 loss impairs innate immunity kinetics
comments: Clever genomic approach revealing functional redundancy in protein synthesis genes; the biological relevance of Aimp1-dependent autophagy enhancement during immune responses needs deeper mechanistic exploration.

model_io.model: mammalian cell lines, murine myeloid cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aimp1 redundancy in protein synthesis allows uncoupling of translation and autophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Aimp1 depletion increases autophagy without decreasing protein synthesis

title: International testing and refinement of AI algorithms predicting acute leukemia subtypes from routine laboratory data
task: International validation and refinement of AI for acute leukemia subtype prediction from laboratory data
key_results: Pretrained algorithm showed variable accuracy (AUROC 0.94 for AML, 0.84 for ALL) with high exclusion rate (70.8–92.5%); ensemble approach (Isolation Forest + LOF) improved AML AUROC from 0.72 to 0.84 while excluding only 12.1%
comments: Large international cohort (6206 patients, 20 centers) is valuable; ensemble method addresses confidence threshold trade-off, though pediatric retraining suggests model needs tuning per population.

model_io.model: Ensemble AI (Isolation Forest + Local Outlier Factor)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble anomaly detection improves generalization of pretrained leukemia-prediction models across international cohorts
hypotheses.result: Ensemble approach improved AML detection while reducing patient exclusion from 70.8–92.5% to 12.1%

title: High-resolution phage-host assignment through key proteins using large language models
task: High-resolution phage-host assignment using protein language models and specialized proteins
key_results: VirHost Hunter doubled phage-host assignments in gut microbiome vs baselines; created gut phage lysin database; synthesized lysin targeting obesity-promoting bacterium
comments: Protein language models effectively capture functional homology without sequence similarity; practical therapeutic application (obesity-targeting lysin) is promising.

model_io.model: VirHost Hunter (Protein Language Models + Vision Transformers)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Protein language models capture functional homology in phage tails/lysins despite sequence dissimilarity, enabling host assignment
hypotheses.result: VirHost Hunter doubled host assignments and identified previously uncharacterized phages

title: Ultra-precision deconvolution of spatial transcriptomics decodes immune heterogeneity and fate-defining programs in tissues
task: Deconvolution of spatial transcriptomics to identify immune cell subpopulations and intercellular programs
key_results: UCASpatial identified pro-fibrotic community (Igfbp5+ chondrocytes, Cd36+ Gpnmb+ macrophages, Fmod+ fibroblasts) in scarring wounds; IL11-IL11RA signaling drives this community and limits regeneration
comments: Entropy-based weighting effectively distinguishes transcriptionally heterogeneous cell subpopulations, though functional validation of identified programs remains limited.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IL11-IL11RA signaling within the pro-fibrotic community drives scarring and limits regeneration
hypotheses.result: Identified pro-fibrotic triad and its key signaling axis in murine wound healing

title: A disease-agnostic approach to ensemble learning for infectious disease forecasting
task: Disease-agnostic ensemble weighting for infectious disease forecasting without historical data
key_results: epiFFORMA outperforms equal-weighting ensembling on COVID-19, dengue, influenza, and 7 other diseases; performs better than individual component models on average
comments: Synthetic data approach is elegant for emerging diseases, but real-world applicability depends on whether synthetic epidemic dynamics faithfully represent novel pathogens.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disease-agnostic weights learned from synthetic epidemiological data outperform equal-weighting on emerging diseases
hypotheses.result: epiFFORMA improved forecasting across 10 infectious diseases compared to naive ensembling

title: Intrinsic mechanisms and microenvironmental cues fine-tune plasticity of esophageal progenitors
task: Characterize esophageal progenitor plasticity regulation via Sox9 and epithelial-stromal communication
key_results: Hedgehog regulates Sox9 indirectly via epithelial-stromal TGF-β/BMP signaling and directly via cell-autonomous Cox-2 mechanism; Cox-2 inhibitors suppress Hedgehog-induced Sox9 in mouse and human models
comments: Identifies both stromal and epithelial mechanisms; Cox-2 inhibition offers tractable therapeutic angle for preventing acid-reflux-associated metaplasia.

model_io.model: Esophageal organoid + mouse model + human explants

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cox-2 mediates Hedgehog-induced esophageal progenitor plasticity via epithelial cell-intrinsic mechanisms
hypotheses.result: Cox-2 inhibitors suppressed Hedgehog-induced Sox9 in both mouse and human models

title: Network model for alignment, stitching and slice-to-volume 3D reconstruction of large-scale spatially resolved slices
task: Slice alignment and 3D reconstruction of spatial-omics data
key_results: GEASO outperforms baselines across multiple platforms and modalities for alignment and 3D reconstruction of tissue slices
comments: Graph neural networks effectively capture topological structure for elastic registration, though scalability to very large datasets remains to be demonstrated in practice.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph neural networks can learn consistent spot features to enable elastic registration across slices
hypotheses.result: GEASO successfully aligned and reconstructed slices across various platforms and modalities

title: Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life
task: Framework for generalist AI modeling DNA, RNA, proteins, and cellular systems
key_results: Synthesis of advances in biological AI for interpreting/generating DNA, RNA, proteins, cellular systems; identified opportunities in synergizing language/structural models and AI agents
comments: Ambitious review articulates vision for generalist biological AI; however, challenges in validation, scalability, and data quality require substantial progress before realizing full potential.

title: Remote monitoring of heart failure exacerbations using a smartwatch
task: Predict heart failure exacerbations from smartwatch-derived daily peak oxygen uptake
key_results: Wearable-derived pVO2 strongly correlated with CPET-measured pVO2 (r=0.85); 10% drop in pVO2 associated with 3.62-fold HR for unplanned healthcare events (median 7.4 d lead time); external validation confirmed findings
comments: 7.4-day lead time is clinically actionable; external validation on All of Us data is valuable, though reduced-sensor model showed weaker association (HR 1.32) with 21-day lag.

inputs: Apple Watch sensor data (heart rate, movement, etc.)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Smartwatch-derived daily pVO2 predicts heart failure exacerbations earlier than static clinical measures
hypotheses.result: 10% pVO2 drop preceded unplanned healthcare events by median 7.4 days (internal) and 21 days (external)

title: Open and sustainable AI: challenges, opportunities and the road ahead in the life sciences
task: Framework for open, sustainable, and reproducible AI in life sciences
key_results: Mapped >300 ecosystem components; provided implementation pathways for reproducible, reusable AI addressing trust and environmental sustainability challenges
comments: Comprehensive perspective addresses critical issues but is primarily a roadmap; quantitative validation of improvements remains future work.

title: LazySlide: accessible and interoperable whole-slide image analysis
task: Whole-slide image analysis and multimodal integration with omics data
key_results: LazySlide enables tissue/cell segmentation, feature extraction, and zero-shot classification leveraging vision-language models; integrates with scverse ecosystem
comments: Built on scverse standards for interoperability, though performance benchmarking against specialized histopathology tools is not provided.

title: Backpropagation-free spiking neural networks with the forward–forward algorithm
task: Training spiking neural networks with forward-forward algorithm as backpropagation alternative
key_results: FF-SNN surpasses existing FF-SNNs on static datasets (MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, K-MNIST); outperforms other SNNs on spiking task (SHD); competitive with BP-trained SNNs while improving biological plausibility
comments: Forward-forward offers better neuromorphic hardware compatibility and layer-wise localization; performance gains are modest but biological plausibility is significant advantage.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Forward-forward algorithm trains SNNs efficiently without backpropagation, maintaining accuracy comparable to BP-SNNs
hypotheses.result: FF-SNN achieved competitive accuracy on both static and spiking datasets while improving biological plausibility

title: Meta-analysis, WGCNA, and machine learning converge on a four-gene biomarker panel for heat stress tolerance in Solanum lycopersicum
task: Meta-analysis integration to identify heat stress tolerance biomarkers in tomato
key_results: Four-gene signature (Solyc09g074500, Solyc01g102960, Solyc07g042230, Solyc02g091990) achieved 98.5% classification accuracy and AUC >0.95; linked to chaperone induction and reduced ethylene/growth pathways
comments: Excellent classification performance and functional coherence; validation in independent heat-stress field trials would strengthen practical utility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integration of meta-DEGs and WGCNA identifies robust heat-tolerance biomarkers
hypotheses.result: Four-gene signature achieved 98.5% classification accuracy with high AUC values

title: Statistical models to characterize colon tumor stiffness heterogeneity through representative atomic force microscopy maps
task: Link tissue stiffness heterogeneity to clinical and phenotypic parameters in colon cancer via AFM and machine learning
key_results: AFM-derived stiffness associated with age, tumor stage, RAS mutations, colon localization, and microsatellite instability; machine learning inferred clinical parameters from stiffness maps
comments: Integrative approach is novel; however, sample size and generalization across tumor types are not detailed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tumor stiffness heterogeneity correlates with clinical parameters including RAS mutations and microsatellite instability
hypotheses.result: Statistical modeling identified known and novel associations; machine learning successfully inferred clinical parameters from stiffness

title: Exploring exosome-related genes as candidate biomarkers in primary immune thrombocytopenia through transcriptomics and preliminary experimental validation
task: Identify exosome-related genes as biomarkers in primary immune thrombocytopenia
key_results: GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, and GSR identified as candidate biomarkers; downregulated in ITP patients (P <0.05); molecular docking suggests drug-binding feasibility; HIBADH requires further validation
comments: Bioinformatic predictions are supported by RT-qPCR, but functional roles in ITP pathogenesis and therapeutic potential remain speculative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosome-related genes regulate immune-megakaryocyte crosstalk in ITP
hypotheses.result: GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, GSR downregulated in ITP; docking suggests drug binding; HIBADH requires further validation

title: Identification and verification of the key genes, CCR1 and EGR2, in diabetes-associated lipophagy
task: Identification of lipophagy-associated biomarkers in diabetes via integrated WGCNA and machine learning
key_results: CCR1 and EGR2 identified as key hub genes; both significantly upregulated in diabetic samples and serum; CCR1-/- mice showed improved glucose homeostasis
comments: Functional validation in mice is valuable; however, only two genes were mechanistically validated, limiting insights into lipophagy pathway biology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCR1 and EGR2 are key lipophagy-associated genes dysregulated in diabetes
hypotheses.result: Both genes significantly upregulated in diabetic samples; CCR1 knockout improved glucose homeostasis in mice

title: Visualization and simulation of full-scale point-neuron circuits via the Neural Circuit Visualizer web platform
task: Simulation and visualization of full-scale point-neuron circuits in hippocampal CA1
key_results: Neural Circuit Visualizer integrates anatomical reconstruction, region-specific connectivity, and HPC resources; enables public simulation and visualization of full-scale neural models
comments: Web-based platform increases accessibility; open-source design encourages community contribution, though computational scalability limits are not detailed.

title: A convolutional attention model classifies copy number variants from whole exome sequencing
task: CNV classification from whole-exome sequencing using convolutional neural networks with attention
key_results: CNN-Att achieved macro F1=0.83, macro PR-AUC=0.93 on held-out test; F1 up to 0.96 on cross-platform validation (HiSeq, NovaSeq, MGISEQ, BGISEQ); robust across sequencing technologies
comments: Strong cross-platform performance is valuable for clinical use; attention mechanism effectively highlights weak depth signals in small/noisy events.

model_io.model: CNN-Att (dual-input convolutional neural network with attention)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Attention-based CNNs generalize better than read-depth heuristics across sequencing platforms
hypotheses.result: CNN-Att achieved consistent F1 >0.8 and cross-platform F1 up to 0.96

title: Optimization of cross-institutional medical federated learning framework driven by confidential computing
task: Multi-objective federated learning accounting for TEE side-channel privacy risks
key_results: CC-PGD reduces privacy leakage by 23–31% and communication cost by 18–27% vs baselines while maintaining within-2% accuracy of centralized training; O(1/√T) convergence proof
comments: First framework to explicitly model TEE vulnerabilities in federated learning; theoretical guarantees are strong, but real-world threat model assumptions require validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint optimization of privacy leakage (including TEE risks) and communication cost improves federated learning robustness
hypotheses.result: CC-PGD achieved better privacy-communication tradeoffs than baselines on IID and non-IID data

title: Spontaneous aging-associated inflammation and genome instability in the immune system of turquoise killifish
task: Characterize aging-associated inflammation and genome instability in turquoise killifish immune system
key_results: Old killifish display increased inflammation markers and DNA damage in immune progenitors vs young; age-related immune changes occur within <10 weeks, validating killifish as aging model
comments: Rapid aging model is advantageous for mechanistic studies; single-cell data provides cell-state resolution, though link between immune aging and systemic aging requires further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Killifish undergo rapid age-dependent immune system changes paralleling human immune aging
hypotheses.result: Old killifish displayed increased inflammation and DNA damage in immune progenitors within <10 weeks

title: Design of robust networks via reinforcement learning prompts the emergence of multi-backbones
task: Design robust networks against multidimensional attacks via RL
key_results: Framework surpasses existing methods for given cost; discovery of multi-backbone topologies during design process
comments: Promising practical application but lacks theoretical convergence guarantees or complexity analysis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Networks develop suitable multi-backbones to mitigate vulnerability during RL-driven design
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through design process observations

title: High-entropy solid electrolytes discovery: a dual-stage machine learning framework bridging atomic configurations and ionic transport properties
task: High-entropy solid electrolyte discovery via dual-stage ML: MLIPs + feature-property mapping
key_results: Framework identifies promising HE-LZSP composition with 4.53 mS/cm ionic conductivity from 4575 candidates; fine-tuned CHGNet + SF-MSD model
comments: Novel dual-stage accelerated discovery approach but MLIP fine-tuning procedure unclear; validation on other SE families not demonstrated despite claimed extensibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-tuned MLIP + interpretable feature-property mapping identifies high-conductivity HE-SEs more efficiently than trial-and-error in high-dimensional composition space
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 4.53 mS/cm composition identified with computational efficiency

title: High-Resolution Downscaled CMIP6 Projections dataset of Key Climate Variables for Senegal
task: High-resolution downscaled CMIP6 climate projections for Senegal
key_results: 0.0375° × 0.0375° resolution daily data from 19 climate models; five essential variables across 1850–2100 and three emissions scenarios
comments: Valuable climate data product but primarily a data processing/quality control contribution; no novel downscaling or validation methodology presented.

outputs: high-resolution (0.0375°) daily temperature, precipitation, radiation for Senegal

title: Performance analysis of network automation techniques for dense IP networks
task: Performance analysis of network automation tools for dense IP networks
key_results: Automated topology creation 4.5× faster than manual; configuration generation ~10% improvement; test execution 11× faster than manual testing
comments: Practical network ops comparison but limited to three topology tools and one config generator; lacks scalability analysis for very large networks.

model_io.model: EVE-NG, Pllama, ContainerLAB topology creators + Komodo/Python config + CLASSIC/MD/NETCONF testing

hypotheses.hypothesis: Automated topology, configuration, and testing tools provide significant time and effort reduction vs. manual approaches in dense IP networks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 4.5–11× speedups observed

title: Proactive fault prediction in marine diesel engines using multivariate machine learning
task: Proactive fault prediction in marine diesel engines via multivariate ConvLSTM
key_results: ConvLSTM decreases RMSE 15.45% vs. decision tree; random forest achieves 82.168% accuracy
comments: Practical predictive maintenance application but accuracy modest; limited to 4-stroke engine; comparison to LSTM, GRU, Transformer lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ConvLSTM captures multivariate temporal dependencies in engine metrics better than decision tree for RMSE prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 15.45% RMSE reduction

title: Sampled-data control under time-varying delays: a robust approach for high-renewable smart grids
task: Robust sampled-data adaptive control for renewable smart grids under time-varying delays
key_results: Adaptive controller reduces settling time 33%, overshoot 52%, energy cost 40% vs. fixed-gain baselines under stochastic impairments
comments: Rigorous LMI-based stability analysis with practical microgrid validation, but evaluation limited to hybrid systems; generalization to larger grids unclear.

model_io.model: Adaptive sampled-data control with delay-jitter scheduling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive feedback gain scheduling via online-measurable communication quality index preserves exponential stability under delay-jitter-packet-loss
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 33% settling time reduction and cyber-aware reliability maintained

title: Comparative evaluation of YOLO and RT-DETR models for real-time defect detection in wood-based 3D printing
task: Real-time defect detection in wood-based 3D printing via YOLO and RT-DETR comparison
key_results: YOLOv11 achieves highest precision (0.632); RT-DETR highest mAP (0.563) and recall (0.581); YOLOv8 optimal latency-accuracy trade-off (3.2 ms)
comments: Practical additive manufacturing application but dataset small (599 images); limited defect diversity and no deployment results in actual printing system.

inputs: wood-based binder jetting powder-bed images (599 labeled)
outputs: defect bounding boxes and class predictions (6 categories)

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv8 balances inference latency and detection accuracy better than YOLOv11 and RT-DETR for real-time powder-bed monitoring
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 3.2 ms inference with competitive mAP

title: Research on the atomic clock signal denoising method based on the hyperbolic tangent smooth threshold function
task: Atomic clock signal denoising via hyperbolic tangent smooth threshold
key_results: SNR increase 14–26% vs. wavelet methods; RMSE reduction 10–28% vs. soft thresholding across three clock types
comments: Incremental signal processing improvement; limited novelty in threshold design; evaluation on only three clock systems.

model_io.model: EMD + hyperbolic tangent threshold + SURE criterion
outputs: denoised signal with improved SNR and RMSE

hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuous hyperbolic tangent threshold function preserves signal smoothness better than hard/soft while suppressing noise
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10–28% RMSE reduction over soft thresholding

title: Adaptive feature selection with gradient-based relevance for intrusion detection systems
task: Adaptive feature selection with gradient-based relevance for energy system intrusion detection
key_results: AFS improves detection accuracy 24.74% and reduces training time 35% vs. PCA-based methods on CIRA-CIC-DoHBrw-2020
comments: Practical cybersecurity enhancement but limited to single dataset; gradient-based relevance definition unclear; comparison to other feature selection methods incomplete.

model_io.model: Adaptive Feature Selection (AFS) with gradient-based relevance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gradient-based relevance captures context-sensitive malicious patterns in encrypted energy traffic better than statistical relevance alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 24.74% accuracy improvement over PCA

title: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment via physics-guided piecewise-linear nadir surrogates and adaptive virtual inertia
task: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment via surrogate modeling and virtual inertia
key_results: FC-RUC reduces operating cost 24.4% (9-bus) and 27.5% (118-bus) while satisfying frequency constraints; avoids full N-k enumeration
comments: Innovative physics-guided surrogate + adaptive virtual inertia integration, but piecewise-linear nadir surrogate validation limited; scalability to 300+ bus systems untested.

model_io.model: Physics-guided piecewise-linear nadir surrogate + tri-stage robust UC

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-guided surrogate with quantile-based safety calibration enforces frequency constraints more economically than conservative worst-case bounds
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 24–27% cost reduction while meeting all constraints

title: SVDHLA: symmetric variable depth hybrid learning automaton and its application
task: Self-adaptive depth learning automaton for exploration-exploitation trade-off
key_results: SVDHLA outperforms state-of-the-art in cumulative reward and regret; LA-based dropout improves neural network performance over prior methods
comments: Novel variable-depth hybrid LA architecture but evaluation mostly on synthetic environments; neural network dropout application peripheral and underexplored.

model_io.model: SVDHLA (symmetric variable depth hybrid LA) + LA-based dropout

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-adaptive depth adjustment in SVDHLA balances exploration-exploitation better than fixed-depth L_KN,K in non-stationary environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior cumulative reward and regret metrics

title: Information theory and thermal properties of an extended cosine hyperbolic potential model
task: Information-theoretic measures and thermodynamic properties of extended cosine hyperbolic potential
key_results: Fisher information, Shannon entropy derived analytically; Cramér-Rao and BBM inequalities validated; thermodynamic predictions (H, S, G, Cp) match NIST data with minor discrepancies
comments: Sound theoretical treatment with good experimental agreement on small molecules, but model generalization to polyatomic systems and solid-state materials untested.

model_io.model: Extended cosine hyperbolic potential + information theory + thermodynamics
inputs: P₂, K₂, KBr, SiO molecular systems
outputs: molar thermodynamic properties (H, S, G, Cp)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extended cosine hyperbolic potential satisfies information-theoretic inequalities and predicts molecular thermodynamics comparable to experiment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; excellent NIST agreement

title: A hybrid unified decoding analytics system for end-of-life strategies in mass-produced EV battery projects
task: Fuzzy multi-criteria decision framework for EV battery end-of-life investment prioritization
key_results: Cipher fuzzy set model identifies circular value-add and energy efficiency as top criteria; component-level reuse and direct cathode-to-cathode recycling as best strategies
comments: Systematic multi-dimensional prioritization framework but fuzzy set methodology not clearly explained; validation on real investment decisions lacking.

model_io.model: Cipher fuzzy set (CFS) hybrid decision model

hypotheses.hypothesis: CFS-based hybrid decision model with cognitive maps and robust aggregation prioritizes circular and energy-efficient EV battery strategies more reliably than defuzzified methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; circular value-add and energy efficiency identified as critical

title: Adaptive graph signal processing for robust multimodal fusion with dynamic semantic alignment
task: Multimodal fusion via adaptive graph signal processing with dynamic semantic alignment
key_results: AGSP-DSA achieves 95.3% accuracy, 93.6% F1 (CMU-MOSEI); 93.4% accuracy, 91.1% F1 (AVE); robust to missing modality
comments: Strong multimodal fusion results via graph-based spectral filtering, but computational complexity and latency not reported; comparison to recent transformer-based fusion limited.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-graph construction with dynamic semantic attention enables robust multimodal fusion under missing modality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 95.3% accuracy and strong F1 with missing modality robustness

title: Dynamic intrusion detection for internet of drones using glowworm swarm optimization
task: Dynamic intrusion detection for Internet of Drones via bio-inspired GSO
key_results: DIGS-RTD reduces false positives and processing time vs. STFA baseline; demonstrates scalability with IoD diversity
comments: Practical security framework but lacks formal complexity analysis and comparison to deep learning baselines; reproducibility unclear.

model_io.model: Glowworm Swarm Optimization (GSO) for feature selection + classification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic GSO-based feature selection reduces computational load while maintaining detection accuracy in IoD
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior false positive / processing time trade-off

title: Integrated approach for edge coverage enhancement based on IRS phase shift control and AP selection in dense user communication system
task: Edge coverage enhancement via IRS phase shift and AP selection in MIMO systems
key_results: SINR gain of 4 dB cell-center, 2.4 dB cell-edge users; AP selection reduces resource overhead
comments: Comprehensive system integration but lacks comparison to recent learning-based optimization; convergence analysis incomplete.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint IRS phase optimization and AP selection reduces SINR gap between cell-center and edge users
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 2.4 dB edge gain over baselines

title: AI-assisted reliability-based design framework for tunnel concrete linings in weak rocks
task: Reliability-based design of tunnel concrete linings in weak rocks via CCM and AI surrogate
key_results: Risk-consistent design charts linking thickness/strength to β/P_f; FRPC reduces P_f significantly at intermediate thicknesses; surrogate model reproduces trends with high consistency
comments: Integrated framework bridging deterministic and probabilistic design, but FORM validation limited to 2D analysis; FRPC material properties assumed deterministic.

model_io.model: CCM + Monte Carlo sampling + FORM + AI surrogate (data-driven)
outputs: safety factor, reliability index β, failure probability P_f

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-guided CCM combined with probabilistic evaluation and AI surrogate enables efficient risk-consistent lining design
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AI surrogate achieves high consistency with conservative bias

title: Lightweight model LMW-YOLO for small object detection in remote sensing images
task: Lightweight small object detection in remote sensing images
key_results: LMW-YOLO achieves 2.6M parameters while maintaining superior mAP across NWPU, RS-STOD, VisDrone datasets; state-of-the-art trade-off
comments: Well-engineered detection pipeline but limited novelty; CSD strategy and module design are incremental improvements over YOLO baseline.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stage-specific feature extraction (CSD) with LKCA for long-range dependencies improves small target detection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via superior mAP on aerial datasets

title: Multi-center chameleon hashing based Blockchain integrated digital copyright transaction scheme for data redacting in Blockchain based IoT systems
task: Blockchain-based digital copyright transaction with chameleon hashing and data redaction
key_results: Scheme satisfies data confidentiality, malicious node resistance, and private key accountability; efficient node exit via flexible key broadcast
comments: Practical blockchain application but security analysis informal; no formal privacy proofs or performance benchmarks vs. existing copyright systems provided.

model_io.model: Blockchain system with multi-center chameleon hashing + attribute encryption

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-center chameleon hashing enables copyright information redaction while maintaining blockchain integrity and auditability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via security analysis

title: A hybrid framework for global weather forecasting via low-resolution dynamical core and multigrid neural operator
task: Hybrid physics-AI weather forecasting via dynamical core and neural operator
key_results: Performance comparable to state-of-the-art medium-range models with lower training cost; enhanced physical consistency
comments: Effective hybrid approach but evaluation limited to specific dynamical core; scalability claims need empirical validation.

model_io.model: Hybrid framework: low-resolution dynamical core + multigrid neural operator

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multigrid neural operator can augment low-resolution dynamical core without gradient propagation through dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables flexibility in dynamical core choice

title: Observation of partonic flow in proton—proton and proton—nucleus collisions
task: Detect partonic (quark-level) flow in small collision systems via baryon-meson grouping
key_results: First observation of distinctive anisotropic flow grouping in p-Pb and p-p collisions at LHC; consistent with hydrodynamic + quark coalescence model
comments: Extends QGP signatures to small systems, suggesting parton formation in pp collisions; challenges conventional understanding of collectivity scales.

model_io.model: LHC proton-lead and proton-proton collisions + flow analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Baryon-meson flow grouping at intermediate pT is a signature of partonic flowing systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in p-Pb and p-p; described by hydrodynamic + coalescence model

title: Giant magneto-cubic in-plane Hall effect in a nonmagnetic material
task: Giant magneto-cubic in-plane Hall effect in nonmagnetic half-Heusler compound
key_results: In-plane Hall magnitude exceeds all prior reports; 2π/3-periodic oscillation with cubic field dependence (2×π/3 symmetry); extrinsic side jump and skew scattering dominate
comments: Unexpected discovery in nonmagnetic material simplifies interpretation; giant signal and room-temperature operation promise applications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cubic in-plane Hall effect under threefold symmetry arises from extrinsic scattering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: scaling laws and DFT support side jump and skew scattering dominance

title: Confined-hydrogel fluidic memristor crossbar array for neuromorphic computing
task: Scalable fluidic memristor array for neuromorphic computing using confined hydrogel
key_results: 10×10 fluidic memristor array; demonstrates paired-pulse facilitation/depression and spike-rate plasticity; 89.5% MNIST classification accuracy
comments: Confined hydrogel design overcomes previous fabrication bottlenecks; ion-based computing offers energy advantages but long-term stability and scaling beyond 10×10 remain open.

model_io.model: Confined hydrogel fluidic memristor array (10×10) on polyimide micropores

hypotheses.hypothesis: Confined hydrogel enables scalable fabrication and exhibits neuromorphic plasticity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10×10 array fabricated; paired-pulse facilitation/depression observed; 89.5% MNIST accuracy

title: Physics-informed Hamiltonian learning for large-scale optoelectronic property prediction
task: Physics-informed Hamiltonian learning for large-scale optoelectronic property prediction in halide perovskites
key_results: HAMSTER framework predicts quantum Hamiltonians across temperature and compositional variation; accuracy and transferability to systems with tens of thousands of atoms; lower data requirements than pure neural networks
comments: Elegant hybrid: encodes known physics (approximate model) and learns residual corrections with minimal first-principles data; enables interpretable, scalable property prediction.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-informed ML learns Hamiltonian residuals with few reference calculations, improving data efficiency and transferability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: HAMSTER predicts properties across temperature and composition for large systems (10k+ atoms)

title: Grain boundary effects on radiation damage and tritium diffusion in Li–Al–O ceramics from molecular dynamics and experiments
task: Study grain boundary effects on radiation damage and tritium diffusion in Li-Al-O ceramics
key_results: GBs suppress damage in LiAlO₂ via interstitial emission; tritium diffusion at GBs 2-10× faster than bulk; trade-off between damage mitigation and tritium retention
comments: Reveals fundamental GB-mediated competing effects critical for TPBAR microstructural design: lower damage but undesirable tritium acceleration.

inputs: LiAlO₂ and LiAl₅O₈ single-crystal and bicrystal structures

hypotheses.hypothesis: Grain boundaries suppress radiation damage compared to single crystals via interstitial emission
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in LiAlO₂; effect less pronounced in LiAl₅O₈

hypotheses.hypothesis: GBs provide fast tritium diffusion pathways
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 2-10× enhancement relative to bulk diffusion

title: Fermi liquid and isotropic superconductivity of Hund scenario for bilayer nickelates
task: Determine pairing mechanism in bilayer nickelate superconductors via Hund scenario vs hybridization
key_results: Hund scenario yields isotropic s±-wave gap, Fermi liquid normal state, lower Tc than hybridization; differences attributed to distinct low-energy dynamics
comments: Schwinger boson comparison suggests Hund coupling alone insufficient; likely requires multiorbital interplay beyond pure Hund or pure hybridization paradigm.

model_io.model: Dynamic Schwinger boson approach (Hund vs hybridization scenarios)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hund coupling drives superconductivity with isotropic s±-wave pairing
hypotheses.result: Hund scenario gives isotropic gap and Fermi liquid, but lower Tc than experiment suggests Hund alone insufficient

title: Orders-of-magnitude improvement in precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
task: Precision spectroscopy of inner-shell ytterbium clock transition for fundamental physics tests
key_results: ~100-fold improvement in precision over prior reports; sub-10-Hz isotope shift uncertainties; bounds on hypothetical boson mediating electron-neutron force
comments: Magic-wavelength lattice trapping enables coherent Rabi oscillations on challenging inner-shell transition, opening dark matter and Lorentz-violation searches.

model_io.model: Yb atoms in 3D magic-wavelength optical lattice + laser spectroscopy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magic-wavelength lattice eliminates AC Stark shifts and improves coherence on inner-shell transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: ~100-fold precision gain; Rabi oscillations observed

title: Exceptional sensitivity near the bistable transition point of a hybrid quantum system
task: Enhance quantum sensing signal-to-noise ratio via nonlinear bistable transition
key_results: 17-fold SNR enhancement near bistable transition point; exceeds ideal bare electron magnetometer sensitivity
comments: Demonstrates that nonlinearity overcomes fundamental noise amplification at exceptional points, a paradigm shift for quantum sensing.

model_io.model: Hybrid quantum system (NV center + van der Pol oscillator)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinearity-induced bistable transition enables high SNR enhancement without exceptional-point noise amplification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 17-fold improvement and state-of-the-art sensitivity

title: Insilico investigations of the physical properties of hexagonal chalcogenide perovskites CsXS3 (X = Nb, V) for UV optoelectronic devices and photovoltaic applications
task: First-principles investigation of electronic and optical properties of hexagonal chalcogenide perovskites CsXS₃
key_results: Narrow indirect band gaps; isotropic optical absorption in visible-NIR from S-p to transition-metal d transitions; HSE06 reveals band-edge renormalization but internal PBE consistency maintained
comments: Thorough methodological treatment clarifies role of functional choice; optical properties promising but device predictions withheld pending experimental validation.

model_io.model: DFT with PBE-GGA and HSE06 hybrid functional

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid functional (HSE06) significantly renormalizes band edges compared to PBE-GGA
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: pronounced band-edge shifts near Fermi level

title: First positronium lifetime imaging using 52Mn and 55Co with a plastic-scintillator-based PET scanner
task: First positronium lifetime imaging using ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co isotopes with plastic-scintillator PET
key_results: Ortho-positronium lifetime measured in reference materials (polycarbonate, fused silica) and tissues with sub-ns precision; ⁵⁵Co provides cleaner PLI spectra despite shorter half-life; ⁵²Mn suitable but higher background
comments: Pioneering PLI demonstration on tissue samples; ⁵⁵Co preferred for spectral purity, though ⁵²Mn's longer half-life aids coincidence rates; methodology open to future isotope exploration.

model_io.model: J-PET plastic-scintillator scanner with ⁵²Mn/⁵⁵Co positron sources

hypotheses.hypothesis: ⁵²Mn and ⁵⁵Co enable positronium lifetime measurements in tissue via triple coincidence (2×511 keV + prompt γ)
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ⁵⁵Co gives cleaner spectra, ⁵²Mn viable with higher background

title: Mirrorless open cavities enabled by boundary incompatibility between perfect electric conductor and perfect magnetic conductor parallel-plate waveguides
task: Mirrorless electromagnetic confinement via PEC–PMC boundary incompatibility
