key_results: Open-cavity mode gap from PEC–PMC interface enables field confinement without physical mirrors; Q ~ 10² with realistic losses; enhanced magnetic-dipole Purcell factor
comments: Elegant conceptual advance: removes physical mirror constraints, enabling free access for atoms/molecules; practical realization via metallic grooves as artificial magnetic conductor.

model_io.model: PEC–PMC parallel-plate waveguide cavity (metallic grooves emulate PMC)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mode gap from PEC–PMC boundary incompatibility confines fields without mirrors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: open cavity achieves Q ~ 10² and strong field localization

title: A novel double defected ground structures and parasitic patches for enhanced MIMO antenna performance
task: MIMO antenna enhancement via double defected ground structures and parasitic patches
key_results: Impedance bandwidth 1.05 GHz (5.5–6.5 GHz); mutual coupling −32 to −52 dB; ECC < 0.0035, diversity gain > 9.992 dB, MEG −6.2 to −8.5 dB
comments: Demonstrates practical integration of DGS and parasitic patches for C-band MIMO; excellent isolation metrics support high-quality diversity performance.

model_io.model: 4-element MIMO antenna array on FR4 substrate

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGS and parasitic patches reduce mutual coupling and enhance impedance matching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: mutual coupling −32 to −52 dB, IBW 1.05 GHz

title: Study on the deoxygenation characteristics of RP-5 aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing inerting
task: Optimize deoxygenation of RP-5 aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing inerting
key_results: Bubble diameter reduction (2.5→1.0 mm) yields 3.9× increase in oxygen mass transfer coefficient; elevated NEA temperature worsens final performance due to increased gas solubility; optimal thermal management identified
comments: CFD-validated study reveals competitive thermodynamic-kinetic coupling; practical guidance for airborne inerting system design.

model_io.model: Eulerian-Eulerian two-fluid CFD (gas-liquid mass transfer in RP-5)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reducing bubble diameter enhances mass transfer via increased interfacial area
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 3.9× coefficient increase for diameter 2.5→1.0 mm

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher NEA temperature improves molecular diffusion but increases gas solubility, net degrading deoxygenation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: competitive thermodynamic-kinetic effect identified

title: A single diode with integrated photosensing, memory and processing for neuromorphic image sensors
task: Monolithic p-GaN/AlGaN/GaN diode with integrated photosensing, memory, and processing for neuromorphic imaging
key_results: Single diode exhibits photosensitivity (10.45 mA/W), photosynaptic plasticity (up to 122% paired-pulse facilitation), and photo-memory (8 linear states); 3-in-1 diode array demonstrates image denoising and classification without external circuits
comments: Band-structure engineering (AlGaN electron reservoir) cleverly decouples charge trapping from standard p-n operation; compact, energy-efficient neuromorphic imager promising but scalability to large focal-plane arrays needs exploration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AlGaN electron reservoir enables charge-trapping control beyond conventional p-n junction, yielding multifunctional sensing-memory-processing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: single diode demonstrates responsivity, photosynaptic facilitation, and photo-memory in one device

title: Origin of pressure-induced anomalies in the nodal-line ferrimagnet Mn3Si2Te6
task: Explain pressure-induced insulator-to-metal transition and magnetic anomalies in nodal-line ferrimagnet
key_results: DFT calculations + Monte Carlo simulations reproduce ordering temperature evolution with pressure; anomalous Hall conductivity requires extrinsic contributions or electron doping beyond band structure effects
comments: Classical Monte Carlo successfully captures magnetic ordering but fails to fully explain Hall conductivity, suggesting incomplete understanding of extrinsic mechanisms.

model_io.model: Classical Monte Carlo with Heisenberg Hamiltonians

hypotheses.hypothesis: Anomalies in magnetism and Hall conductivity originate from electronic phase transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed for magnetism; Hall conductivity incompletely explained by band structure alone
